scope_note: QA Witt Tower Safe-Haven Null cert [443]. Certified null result: the Witt tower filter bank (cert [439]) produces zero fixed-layer elevation for safe-haven assets (Gold) during NBER recessions, in contrast to cert [442] which shows positive fixed-layer elevation for GSPC/SILSO. COMPANION: M=[[5,-1],[1,0]], p=3, r=1 (det=+1, cert [439]), tower level k=3 (mod 27). GEOMETRIC GROUND TRUTH (C4): fixed-point locus of M mod 27 is exactly {(0,0),(9,9),(18,18)}, satisfying b≡e≡0 mod 9; exhaustively verified over all 729 states of Z/27Z x Z/27Z. MECHANISM: fixed-layer elevation (cert [442]) requires consecutive returns landing at rank bins multiples of 9 (bins 0, 9, 18); crisis sell-off assets (GSPC) land at bins 0-3 on consecutive months (large consecutive negatives); safe-haven assets (Gold) either rally or oscillate and do not cluster at {0,9,18} bins. DOMAIN — Gold (GC=F) Monthly Log-Returns (Yahoo Finance live or hardcoded 2000-2024 fallback): rank-normalize to Z/27Z, state=(rank[t], rank[t-1]). NBER recessions (2001-03/11, 2007-12/2009-06, 2020-02/04). CERTIFIED FACTS: (C1) Gold birth fraction 68.6% in [55%,75%] PASS. (C2) Gold recession fixed_frac=0.0% <=2% PASS. (C3) Permutation null test p=1.000 >=0.15 PASS (strongest possible null: observed delta=0.4pp is minimum achievable). (C4) Fixed-point locus exact match {(0,0),(9,9),(18,18)} PASS. (C5) GFC (2007-12 to 2009-06) mean rank bin=14.2>12 PASS (Gold crisis states in upper half of Z/27Z, away from fixed-point bins). CONTRAST: cert [442] GSPC recession fixed_frac=9.7% (delta=+8.8pp, p=0.015); Gold recession fixed_frac=0.0% (delta=0.4pp, p=1.000). The null is geometrically necessary: safe-haven rally pushes states into bins 18-26, which are not fixed-point bins. Theorem NT compliance: log-returns->rank bins is one-way observer projection; filter bank output never re-enters QA layer as causal input. PRIMARY SOURCES: NBER Business Cycle Dating Committee www.nber.org/cycles; Wall, D.D. (1960) Amer. Math. Monthly 67(6) doi:10.1080/00029890.1960.11989541 (Pisano lifting law underlying cert [439]). Derived and validated 2026-06-18.
